This week, there was a brief news story about a man being murdered in broad daylight, in front of a crowd of people, for his expressions of speech. Then, the story just faded away.
Yes, actually it did. Because I'm not talking about [that](https://substack.com/redirect/0022c5ce-ebe7-49be-9570-497a0e7e5f0b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) murder. I'm talking about a [murder on Main Street](https://substack.com/redirect/d056e581-0b5f-4862-b386-3ddacf04adba?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), thousands of miles from Utah, in Crossville, Tennessee. A murder that I would argue is _even more emblematic_ of where we are politically as a country right now, and that demands as much thought and analysis as the one the media can't stop talking about.
Lucas's Substack is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9sdWNhc2t1bmNlLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.AUOEQmot6wSW1mObHPokPqvhuqyO8rnp9BeriPPdDMU?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=173516190)
Albert Sturgill, Jr., was the type of guy many of us know from our own hometowns across the country. You'll probably understand what I mean when I say that, on August 22nd, he was conducting one of his usual activities: pacing back and forth alone in front of the Cumberland County Veterans Memorial muttering to himself and sporadically [flipping his middle finger at the monument](https://substack.com/redirect/f32ebedc-0541-4912-a036-14f6ff20f97a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Something Sturgill was well known for in the community.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/da36a284-1b62-4fa3-a264-fa389f2912b3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Cumberland County Veterans Memorial Park, Crossville, TN
Only this time, for whatever reason, rather than leaving him alone, some passers-by decided to stop and teach him a lesson in patriotism.
The first, Eric Garrison, apparently felt particularly disrespected and compelled to engage Sturgill because - while neither he, nor his father, nor his grandfather had ever served - his _great_ -grandfather had gotten a purple heart in World War II...
As Garrison lectured Sturgill on respect and how "these people died for you," a second man jumped in and began cursing angrily at Sturgill. Sturgill, for his part, according to Garrison's wife, didn't respond.
As the crowd grew, Garrison, the second man, and a third began closing in on Sturgill. When he was closer, Garrison noticed that Sturgill had a "stick" tied to his waist, ran around behind him, and tried to take it from him.
Sturgill, a 62 year old disabled man with a prosthetic foot, finally responded by struggling with the 42 year old Garrison to keep possession of his walking aid. Which he ultimately retained by yanking it back and hitting Garrison over the head with it.
Video footage, [according to NBC news](https://substack.com/redirect/f32ebedc-0541-4912-a036-14f6ff20f97a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), shows Sturgill backing up from the three men and waving his arms while Garrison, now incensed, runs off camera back to his car, grabs a firearm, returns- with his wife physically clinging to him and trying to hold him back and stop him-and shoots Sturgill at point blank range. Police sirens approach in the background and the three men just walk away, leaving Sturgill lying there to bleed out.
Larry Doster, a Vietnam veteran who owns the shop across the street summarized it like this: "I don't care what he was doing. He didn't deserve to be shot. He had every right to object to whatever he wanted to, even though I don't agree with it."
This, of course, is the great irony of the situation. And even the murderer, Garrison, recognized it. According to Garrison's wife, Garrison started the confrontation with the words: "I know this is a free country. I know you have freedom of speech, but what you're doing is wrong. These people died for you."
Garrison knew, in his heart, that the men who were memorialized at that monument died for Sturgill's right to free speech, and that he, Garrison, was doing wrong by trying to stifle it. But he did it anyway.
**Because, right now, for many in our country like Garrison, symbols of freedom have become more important than freedom itself.**
The substance of things no longer matters to them. They have embraced a new spirit of symbolic freedom in lieu of the foundational freedoms our country was founded on-- even when the two directly contradict one another.
You can see that every day in our politics. In the lore of our country, when rights were more important than symbols of those rights, the prize of winning in politics was the opportunity to manage the country and make decisions about the best way forward. Who those decisions were best for, or whether they were good or bad decisions, was always up for debate, but the people in charge were at least nominally "managing" things.
But management isn't sexy and it doesn't get you TV coverage or clicks. So in the last decade of hyper-charged attention and social media, and perhaps even as a direct result of earlier mismanagement by those we trusted, many people and politicians have accepted a new end goal of politics: winning itself.
And, with the media profiting over every single play and churning out 24/7 coverage, every day has become a new game in the never-ending season of politics.
It might sound cynical, but what we are seeing right now is what happens when politics is reduced to winning and losing.
A few days ago, a friend of mine asked me if I thought the murder of Charlie Kirk was "a professional hit." Another friend asked me a similar question the day of the UnitedHealthcare murder, which I consider a political killing.
My answer for both the Kirk and UnitedHealthcare murders was that no, I didn't think they were "professional" hits (which was subsequently confirmed in both cases).
First of all, I don't think there's such a thing as professional hits like most people imagine from the movies. There aren't legions of professional murderers for hire running around and, if there were, they wouldn't be doing it with firearms from rooftops in broad daylight.
Furthermore, what we are witnessing right now isn't refined. It's a symptom of this dark win-at-all-costs political era woven with division, fear, anger, inequality, and desperation. In that mess, motivated individuals, inspired by their beliefs, or perhaps just by the belief that they are irrelevant, feel compelled to jump headlong into America's newest blood sport.
There are dozens of articles recapping last year's political violence, mentioning things like the presidential assassination attempts, state lawmaker murders in Minnesota, firebombing of the governor's house in Pennsylvania, a Tesla truck firework bomb set ablaze, shootings at campaign offices and the CDC, the list goes on.
We've all seen how crazy fans get at football games. Imagine if, just for a second, fans had the opportunity do more than just yell, but actually get into the game and impact whether their team would "win" that day. How many of the 80,000 roaring Sunday afternoon football fans would push a secret button under their seat if it meant that it would take out one of the opposing team's players? How many would push that same button to kill an opposing player?
It only takes one.
And with politics in America raging every single day, all day, and millions and millions of fans who have much more on the line than who wins or loses on a Sunday afternoon, it's no surprise that things keep happening.
Particularly because this change in atmosphere has twisted people's minds and reduced other people to one of two things: teammates or opponents.
All of the quotes I've read about Charlie Kirk's killer were some variation of the line that he had "only recently become involved in politics." What more impactful way was there for him to contribute to his new team, whether it was right v. right or something else, than to take out what he considered an opposing player?
Looking back, I don't think that Eric Garrison killed Albert Sturgill Jr. because he was flipping off the veterans memorial. I suspect that Garrison killed him because he was ashamed that he'd gotten his red-blooded-9-line-t-shirt-wearing-faux-patriot-ass whooped by a crazy old crippled man in front of a crowd of people.
But he started the fight by revealing how twisted his mind was: acknowledging that Sturgill had a right to free speech, but trying to silence him anyway. And he ultimately didn't blink at killing him, despite his wife clinging to him and pleading with him to stop, because in his mind Sturgill wasn't another person, but an opponent.
The question is, what do we do now?
I see at least two ways to approach an atmosphere like this: by recruiting more people to your team, or by changing the paradigm. And while I don't think they are mutually exclusive, the first strategy would have to be carefully coordinated in order to maintain the latter.
I, for one, want to do both, and am curious what you all think-- so please let me know in the comments.
My goal is to start exploring tangible things we can do in the next few weeks. In the meantime, if you'd like to read more about a time when we faced a similar situation and came out of it -- an era when a Congressman was murdered, on average, every 7 years, and two presidents were assassinated -- you can check out my earlier Substack "[On the Brink of Anarchy](https://substack.com/redirect/8799a3c1-7893-4e41-be63-c8ac39c0bc80?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)," or just the excerpt below.
Thank you for reading and supporting, and please share with anyone you think might find it interesting!
-Lucas

* * *
EXCERPT FROM [ON THE BRINK OF ANARCHY](https://substack.com/redirect/8799a3c1-7893-4e41-be63-c8ac39c0bc80?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[In the late 1800's](https://substack.com/redirect/4e7835ef-cf67-457d-840d-7b02b1f3180e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) wealth inequality was skyrocketing, workers were organizing for power against an elite profit-maximizing class, and political polarization was at a zenith. As a result, people caught political fever, and it became contentious. Check out how [familiar this description of the late 1800's sounds](https://substack.com/redirect/4e7835ef-cf67-457d-840d-7b02b1f3180e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg):
_" Such public politics became, in the words of one comedian, "our great American game." Political rancor grew precipitously. Saloons resounded with heated debates. On train cars, Americans took straw polls to see how strangers would vote. At dinner tables, families bonded--or broke up--debating an upcoming race. Even when exhausted Americans threw down their newspapers, they looked up only to find partisan broadsides slathered on every wall. "Ignorance is bliss now," complained one woman as she canceled her political newspapers, weary of the whole spectacle."_
Did you know that the highest sustained participation in elections, something we are seeing a surge in right now, was actually during the late 1800's?
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/c680e026-b141-4980-93cc-92186c4198e7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Elections were also closer than ever before. [No president in this period came to office by winning a majority of the popular vote](https://substack.com/redirect/4e7835ef-cf67-457d-840d-7b02b1f3180e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
At the same time, workers who were tired of being abused started fighting for an 8 hour work week. Anarchism and "[propaganda of the deed](https://substack.com/redirect/d878bf1b-22d6-4d30-aaae-13dacbb125db?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)," like what Mangione and Livelsberger did, proliferated.
And some of them even became martyrs, like Mangione and Livelsberger appear to aspire to. For example, [in 1886](https://substack.com/redirect/641bcd1b-2022-4826-9705-a3d206302e54?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) Chicago, "a rally at Haymarket Square was organized by labor radicals (note: their word, and one often chosen by those who take more than their share to label those who want a fair piece of it) to protest the killing and wounding of several workers by the Chicago police during a strike the day before at the McCormick Reaper Works."

When police arrived at the rally to disperse the crowd, someone threw a bomb at the officers. Mayhem ensued, and in "August 1886, eight men labeled as anarchists were convicted in a sensational and controversial trial in which the jury was considered to be biased and no solid evidence was presented linking the defendants to the bombing."
Most were hanged, but they are remembered, and despite being called anarchists, they are not remembered in a bad light. Quite the opposite, in fact. There is a [memorial to them standing in Chicago today](https://substack.com/redirect/ef198b7d-c705-4dd8-963b-e00ba1a3161c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that is listed on the National Parks Service website.
Political violence was everywhere during this time. On average, a sitting US Congressman was murdered [every seven years](https://substack.com/redirect/4e7835ef-cf67-457d-840d-7b02b1f3180e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Two presidents were assassinated, in 1881 and 1901.
We are there again. Multiple assassination attempts on a (former) President and presidential candidate. Propaganda of the deed proliferating with Mangione and Livelsberger. And political polarization and doubts in democracy everywhere.
Have you heard someone say that democracy is failing because people are stupid? (Come on, you've probably thought that yourself) Or that dumb and uneducated people have more children than smart people so we are doomed?
Here is a quote from an [1878 treatise on the failure of democracy](https://substack.com/redirect/d6717737-9da3-44da-a302-96ad636b1750?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by Francis Parkman, an elite of the era, bemoaning the growing democratic power of the lower classes:
_" It is aggravated by the fact, generally acknowledged "by those most competent to judge of it," that intellectual development and high civilization are not favorable to fecundity, so that the unintelligent classes, except when in actual destitution, multiply faster than those above them. Thus the power of ignorance tends to increase, or rather the power of the knaves who are always at hand to use it."_
Jon Grinspan, of the National Museum of American History, [whose article](https://substack.com/redirect/4e7835ef-cf67-457d-840d-7b02b1f3180e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) I quoted a couple of times above, lists even more similarites:
_" Others attacked the rising immigration rates, like Francis Willard, the leader of the Women's Christian Temperance Union, who blamed America's out-of-control politics on "alien illiterates." Others still aimed (more accurate) attacks at railroads, corporations, robber barons and lobbyists who seemed to be buying up America. The muckraking reporter Henry Demarest Lloyd wrote that "liberty produces wealth, and wealth destroys liberty."_
So how did this era end and is there anything we can learn from it?
According to Jon, the political and voting fervor began to die down as those in charge stifled voting access in order to decrease turnout. Which, you can see in the turnout chart above, appears to have had the intended result.
At the same time, however, labor unions started winning 8 hour workdays left and right, antitrust laws were passed and enforced, and a feeling of fairness started to return the country. In short, normal people started getting results and maybe weren't as interested in politics anymore.
So, if we want to move on from this, and not fall into twenty years of political strife and violence, perhaps the best way to do that is to make things fair again. To get big money out of politics and government, to pass laws so that insurance companies can't [reject bionic arms for little girls](https://substack.com/redirect/b5d121e4-bcd5-4a38-9b24-5b8ad8f0d00b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and otherwise bring a sense of fairness back to America.
You're currently a free subscriber to [Lucas's Substack](https://substack.com/redirect/a9552b28-76fa-42f7-a6e9-b7d7fefe1d5f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). For the full experience, [upgrade your subscription.](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9sdWNhc2t1bmNlLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.AUOEQmot6wSW1mObHPokPqvhuqyO8rnp9BeriPPdDMU?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=postcta)
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9sdWNhc2t1bmNlLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.AUOEQmot6wSW1mObHPokPqvhuqyO8rnp9BeriPPdDMU?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=postcta)

In the meantime, if you'd like to read more about a time when we faced a similar situation and came out of it -- an era when a Congressman was murdered, on average, every 7 years, and two presidents were assassinated -- you can check out my earlier Substack "," or just the excerpt below. Thank you for reading and supporting, and please share with anyone you think might find it interesting! -Lucas Lucas's Substack is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. * EXCERPT FROM wealth inequality was skyrocketing, workers were organizing for power against an elite profit-maximizing class, and political polarization was at a zenith. As a result, people caught political fever, and it became contentious. Check out how : " Such public politics became, in the words of one comedian, "our great American game." Political rancor grew precipitously. Saloons resounded with heated debates. On train cars, Americans took straw polls to see how strangers would vote. At dinner tables, families bonded--or broke up--debating an upcoming race. Even when exhausted Americans threw down their newspapers, they looked up only to find partisan broadsides slathered on every wall. "Ignorance is bliss now," complained one woman as she canceled her political newspapers, weary of the whole spectacle." Did you know that the highest sustained participation in elections, something we are seeing a surge in right now, was actually during the late 1800's? | | ---|---|--- Elections were also closer than ever before. . At the same time, workers who were tired of being abused started fighting for an 8 hour work week. Anarchism and "," like what Mangione and Livelsberger did, proliferated. And some of them even became martyrs, like Mangione and Livelsberger appear to aspire to. For example, Chicago, "a rally at Haymarket Square was organized by labor radicals (note: their word, and one often chosen by those who take more than their share to label those who want a fair piece of it) to protest the killing and wounding of several workers by the Chicago police during a strike the day before at the McCormick Reaper Works." When police arrived at the rally to disperse the crowd, someone threw a bomb at the officers. Mayhem ensued, and in "August 1886, eight men labeled as anarchists were convicted in a sensational and controversial trial in which the jury was considered to be biased and no solid evidence was presented linking the defendants to the bombing." Most were hanged, but they are remembered, and despite being called anarchists, they are not remembered in a bad light. Quite the opposite, in fact. There is a that is listed on the National Parks Service website. Political violence was everywhere during this time. On average, a sitting US Congressman was murdered . Two presidents were assassinated, in 1881 and 1901. We are there again. Multiple assassination attempts on a (former) President and presidential candidate. Propaganda of the deed proliferating with Mangione and Livelsberger. And political polarization and doubts in democracy everywhere. Have you heard someone say that democracy is failing because people are stupid? (Come on, you've probably thought that yourself) Or that dumb and uneducated people have more children than smart people so we are doomed? Here is a quote from an by Francis Parkman, an elite of the era, bemoaning the growing democratic power of the lower classes: " It is aggravated by the fact, generally acknowledged "by those most competent to judge of it," that intellectual development and high civilization are not favorable to fecundity, so that the unintelligent classes, except when in actual destitution, multiply faster than those above them. Thus the power of ignorance tends to increase, or rather the power of the knaves who are always at hand to use it." Jon Grinspan, of the National Museum of American History, I quoted a couple of times above, lists even more similarites: " Others attacked the rising immigration rates, like Francis Willard, the leader of the Women's Christian Temperance Union, who blamed America's out-of-control politics on "alien illiterates." Others still aimed (more accurate) attacks at railroads, corporations, robber barons and lobbyists who seemed to be buying up America. The muckraking reporter Henry Demarest Lloyd wrote that "liberty produces wealth, and wealth destroys liberty." So how did this era end and is there anything we can learn from it? According to Jon, the political and voting fervor began to die down as those in charge stifled voting access in order to decrease turnout. Which, you can see in the turnout chart above, appears to have had the intended result. At the same time, however, labor unions started winning 8 hour workdays left and right, antitrust laws were passed and enforced, and a feeling of fairness started to return the country. In short, normal people started getting results and maybe weren't as interested in politics anymore. So, if we want to move on from this, and not fall into twenty years of political strife and violence, perhaps the best way to do that is to make things fair again. To get big money out of politics and government, to pass laws so that insurance companies can't , and otherwise bring a sense of fairness back to America. You're currently a free subscriber to . For the full experience, | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Lucas Kunce P.O. Box 1240, Independence, MO 64050 ? ?

# [Why I Filed a Police Report Against Laura Loomer](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4258115&post_id=173517996&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczNTE3OTk2LCJpYXQiOjE3NTc3Nzk4MTUsImV4cCI6MTc2MDM3MTgxNSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQyNTgxMTUiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.wpghDS8o8jFG02tfraqRAzJdXTdGTSeFkCesnsx6cFs)
### She threatened my son, but it won't work.
| | [Mike Nellis](https://substack.com/@mikenellis)
---
| Sep 13
---
| [](https://substack.com/@mikenellis)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4258115&post_id=173517996&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczNTE3OTk2LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1Nzc3OTgxNSwiZXhwIjoxNzYwMzcxODE1LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNDI1ODExNSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.QGIGnlvV-ELLQkwHg7u4SE4fPMPP2gf4hJRH0J9Cn0s&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4258115&post_id=173517996&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczNTE3OTk2LCJpYXQiOjE3NTc3Nzk4MTUsImV4cCI6MTc2MDM3MTgxNSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQyNTgxMTUiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.wpghDS8o8jFG02tfraqRAzJdXTdGTSeFkCesnsx6cFs&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4258115&post_id=173517996&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczNTE3OTk2LCJpYXQiOjE3NTc3Nzk4MTUsImV4cCI6MTc2MDM3MTgxNSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQyNTgxMTUiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.wpghDS8o8jFG02tfraqRAzJdXTdGTSeFkCesnsx6cFs)
---
| | [](https://substack..tAvZsPNN7dO5MqBXLcVas4LTJONQNsfcdpOj_BNvG8U?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/endlessurgency/p/why-i-filed-a-police-report-against?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

As many of you probably saw, last night I filed a police report and had the Chicago Police Department at my house after Laura Loomer--one of Donald Trump's closest advisors--sent me a threatening message on Twitter that mentioned both me and my son:
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ddb80282-7dc6-4e8b-b059-ae359f3260c3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Loomer has been obsessed with me for years. Almost exactly a year ago, she doxxed my home address and encouraged her followers to come find me. Over the last 48 hours, she's called me a demon, blamed me for Charlie Kirk's murder, and told her supporters that I "should not know a moment's peace."
Given what happened to Charlie Kirk and Melissa Hortman--and the rising tide of violent rhetoric from the far right targeting Democrats--I felt I had no choice but to report her. What comes of it, I don't know. In this country, it's hard to hold people accountable for this kind of language. But I'm doing what I need to do to protect my family, just like I did last year when Loomer doxxed me.
We're in a dangerous moment. Political violence is escalating. The rhetoric on the right is dangerous and unacceptable--and now they're using Kirk's murder as a pretext to come after people like me.
What we need are serious adults in positions of power who can tone down the rhetoric, take real action, and actually try to keep people safe. That means addressing gun control. That means expanding access to mental health care. That means securing soft spaces like schools--and having a serious, honest conversation about the epidemic of lonely, isolated men being radicalized online.
Here's the truth: Loomer keeps coming after me because I've been one of her most vocal critics. I've called out her, Trump, and this entire administration. I've done it in their safe spaces--on Fox News, Newsmax, and more--and I'm not stopping. She thought a threatening private message would make me shut up. Instead, I screenshotted it and posted it for the world to see. Because I don't get bullied. I don't back down. I'm going to protect my family, for sure--and I'm going to keep standing up for what I believe in.
One day, when my son's grown--and if we've made it through this insane period--I want to be able to look him in the eye and say: I did everything I could. I fought. I helped give him, and his generation, a shot at something better than what's happening in America today. That I gave a damn, and I didn't give in or abandon my values.
I want to thank everyone who's sent kind messages. The support has meant the world. Thanks to my Chicago community, to my friends, and to the local officials who responded quickly. And thank you to the Chicago Police Department for showing up, securing my home, and taking this seriously.
Don't expect to see less of me. Expect to see more.
I will keep calling Laura Loomer what she is. And I will keep working to hold her accountable for the rhetoric I believe helped fuel Charlie Kirk's murder. While they scramble to pin this on Democrats, there's growing evidence the shooter may have been radicalized by the far right--by people like Loomer and Nick Fuentes, who were calling Charlie Kirk a traitor, a globalist, a fascist, and worse.
I'm not going anywhere. I'll keep doing the work. I'll keep showing up. I just wanted you to know that I'm okay--and that I appreciate you.
It's been a hard week. We've been at each other's throats--Democrats, Republicans, everyone. Please, take some time this weekend to breathe. Hug your kid. Call your mom. Watch some college football (which is exactly what I'm about to do). Take care of yourself.
We need you in this fight, too.

_Mike Nellis is a Democratic strategist and entrepreneur who has raised over $1 billion for Democratic campaigns and causes. He 's the founder of Authentic.org, an award-winning fundraising and advertising agency, and a former Senior Advisor to Vice President Kamala Harris. This Substack is where he writes with endless urgency about the issues of the day--and how we can save the Democratic Party and our democracy._

I wanted to take a moment to reach out -- not with a breaking deadline, but with a quick note about where things stand as we move through September. Our end-of-quarter fundraising deadline is just a few weeks away. It’s an important checkpoint for our campaign, and it’s one of the key moments that set the tone for the months ahead and shows the strength of this grassroots team. I know how much people have on their plates right now, and that’s part of why I’m so grateful. Because this grassroots team shows up with consistency, with heart, and with a real sense of what matters most to folks across Central and Southern Illinois.

name: Randy Villegas
subject: SCOTUS just greenlit racial profiling in LA. We need to act.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Villegas for Congress [#]
body:
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP62/1/117525/images/Villegas%20Logo.png]
On Monday, in a 6-3 decision, the Supreme Court cleared the way for sweeping immigration operations in Los Angeles. The ruling lifts limits on roving stops that a federal judge said swept up even U.S. citizens.

https://secure.ngpvan.com/7so6CgsS50-jSA0QmRp7Hg2 [https://secure.ngpvan.com/7so6CgsS50-jSA0QmRp7Hg2] Supreme Court lifts restrictions on LA immigration stops set after agents swept up US citizens

Here’s what that means: federal agents can proceed with broad stops while the case continues, and officials are already promising to “flood the zone.” Our communities are bracing for more raids, fear, and mistakes that hurt working families.

I grew up here in the Central Valley as the son of immigrant parents who worked hard to build a better life. I know what it feels like when families are treated like suspects just because of where they come from, or the language they speak. It’s wrong. It tears at our communities and betrays the promise of this country.

And let’s be clear: this isn’t just about Los Angeles.

If these tactics are allowed there, nothing stops them from spreading across California and reaching right into our neighborhoods in the Central Valley. That is why it is so important we speak out together, right now.

I’ve talked to kids who remember their parents being taken away. I’ve spoken to farmworkers who won’t report wage theft because they’re afraid of being asked about their status.

That fear doesn’t keep us safe – it pushes people further into the shadows.

If you agree, add your name to condemn this ruling and demand protections for our neighbors in California and across the country: [https://secure.ngpvan.com/7so6CgsS50-jSA0QmRp7Hg2]

ADD YOUR NAME [https://secure.ngpvan.com/7so6CgsS50-jSA0QmRp7Hg2]

Thank you,

Randy

Wait, before you go!
Our campaign is corporate-free. No PAC checks, no billionaire donors – just teachers, farmworkers, and neighbors stepping up to fight for justice.
If you can, chip in today to help us power rapid response and stand with families under threat. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rvfc-em-q3-2025?refcode=em-9.13.25-all-a]
Donate $3 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rvfc-em-q3-2025?amount=&refcode=em-9.13.25-all-a] Donate Other Amount [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rvfc-em-q3-2025?refcode=em-9.13.25-all-a]
https://www.facebook.com/VillegasforCongress [https://www.facebook.com/VillegasforCongress] https://www.instagram.com/villegasforcongress/ [https://www.instagram.com/villegasforcongress/] https://x.com/Villegas_CA22 [https://x.com/Villegas_CA22][https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP62/1/117525/images/unnamed-2.png]With deep ties to our community and a lifelong commitment to justice and equity, Randy is running for Congress in California’s 22nd District to bring bold, compassionate leadership to Washington. To support Randy's campaign consider making a grassroots contribution here. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rvc-web-2025?refcode=em-footer]
We'd hate to see you go, but you can click here if you'd like to unsubscribe: https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/YVd1H8iXaku5biXQEwa1sQ2?unsubscribedata=R9PXsChqSzAG%2F11t1txphnpxTBm4SDHho7TO7B%2BHDap3mldRAxkHnJQF0NKaRwjB%2FKTpGmC%2BCF288MUBIVoQ8n6P3FoiU1VOB2HTW1GeyPWe0ZYLZcEW1WXUmwaxidixNvFdgN2SOKlzP06kK6xLYrB%2BGxQo6H0CPrj7QdHD4LfvHAvpiixPysxRktQIot4GoJhCCy5vac3VSTQ%2B9C05wmB7k7aYI6C173tAFt5nnI9xzP514GnJHG%2B%2FrCXilyv4674sMkxNXcHTdlh6nNTPagi4mNUY4gEfo9i84FeNXdaKPQeV%2FspttF511FkrDIhqsf8rEXDEexOmFfwEzpQFAmhl00ZJ0NOWh5WhIsF%2BcTCBbap8h6M7HdRzAuHoaU6tiakgyqv8ELVBC208YzVal9UchAyZw1bzJPBGZpnmVGMyRpjZq7olekB9gHRlRWJX .

body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dasspac-web-2025?refcode=em-header [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dasspac-web-2025?refcode=em-header]
Friend, where are the actions that are Making America Healthy Again?

We’re seriously asking — because what we’ve seen so far is nothing short of dangerous:

* Rollbacks on cancer and disease research.
* The removal of actual medical professionals from federal health agencies.
* Limits on testing for food-borne illnesses that put families at risk.

This isn’t a plan for health. This is sabotage.

“MAHA” is a threat to the health and safety of every American. And at the center of it is Robert F. Kennedy Jr., whose reckless leadership is undermining science, eroding trust, and leaving our communities more vulnerable to disease and preventable illness.

We cannot let this happen. Public health should be guided by science and expertise — not politics, conspiracy theories, or dangerous neglect.

That’s why we’re calling for Robert F. Kennedy Jr. to be removed now.

But to get there, we need a movement of people willing to push back against this reckless agenda and fight for leaders who will protect our families, not put them at risk.

SAY NO TO MAHA [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/partner-swalwell-dass-2025]

The stakes could not be higher. Lives are on the line — and silence is not an option.

Let’s fight for real public health, together.

- Team DASS and Eric Swalwell
Thank you for your dedicated support of The Democratic Association of Secretaries of State -- the only organization dedicated to electing and protecting Democratic Secretaries of States.
Right now, Republicans are changing the rules and changing the players to try and change the outcomes of future elections. Democratic Secretaries of State are the last line of defense to protect our democracy and preserve the will of the voters. They want a blank check to ram through their woke agenda of universal healthcare, the Green New Deal, and every crazy policy you and I have fought against.

name: Squad Victory Fund
subject: Sneakflation is real — and it’s hurting working families.
body:
[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/18910?refcode=em20250913-1520-foote&t=1001&akid=1520%2E342411%2Elj5Y9_ ]Squad Victory Fund

Peter,

Donald Trump promised to lower costs. Instead, his economic policies —
especially his tariffs — are making everyday life more expensive for
millions of Americans.

American consumers are now bearing the brunt of Trump’s tariff policies,
meaning more money out of your pocket for the same groceries, household
goods, clothing, and basic tools.

Even worse, manufacturers are quietly passing along price increases — a
trend economists are calling “sneakflation.” The result? Your bills slowly
creep higher while wages stagnate and corporate profits soar.^1

That’s not protecting the American people — it’s punishing them.

The Squad is fighting for an economy that actually works for the people —
not one rigged by corporate lobbyists and right-wing politicians. We’re
pushing to lower costs and hold leaders accountable. But we can’t do it
alone.

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20250913-movement.4F3SJN&refcode=em20250913-1520&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1002&akid=1520%2E342411%2Elj5Y9_ ]If you’re tired of rising prices, broken promises, and corruption, chip
in $3 to the Squad Victory Fund today. Every dollar helps
us back progressives who are fighting for economic justice.

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20250913-movement.4F3SJN&refcode=em20250913-1520&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1021&akid=1520%2E342411%2Elj5Y9_ ]Contribute ››

In solidarity,

The Squad Victory Fund

P.S. Trump and his allies want you to believe they’re fighting for working
people. But the truth is in your grocery bill. [ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20250913-movement.4F3SJN&refcode=em20250913-1520&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1022&akid=1520%2E342411%2Elj5Y9_ ]Let’s build something
better — together.

1 - [ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/20190?t=1023&akid=1520%2E342411%2Elj5Y9_ ]CNN

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe:

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.squadvictoryfund.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=1520%2E342411%2Elj5Y9_

Paid for by The Squad Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, and Rooted in Community Leadership PAC.

In addition to what’s legally required, The Squad Victory Fund does not accept contributions from: (1) corporations, associations, or their PACs; (2) individuals registered as federal lobbyists or under FARA; (3) from the PACs, lobbyists, or SEC-named executives of fossil fuel companies; or (4) from the Fraternal Order of Police.

Contributions or gifts to The Squad Victory Fund are not tax deductible.

Contributions to The Squad Victory Fund will be allocated equally among: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, and Rooted in Community Leadership PAC.

Any contributor may designate their contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if any contributor makes a contribution that, when allocated, would exceed the amount that the contributor may lawfully give to any participant.

After contributing to The Squad Victory Fund, you will automatically be signed up to receive email updates from Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, and Rashida Tlaib for Congress. You may of course opt-out by unsubscribing at any time.

To contribute via check, please address to The Squad Victory Fund c/o MBA Consulting Group, PO Box 15845, Washington D.C., 20003

Email us: info@squadvictoryfund.com

name: Rashida Tlaib
subject: They’re trying to censure me for speaking out for Palestine
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rashida Tlaib for Congress
body:
This new censure resolution is an attempt to silence me and our movement for peace. | | | [](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.X6XUC7u8ppFLNtMO_55SZlNseb7gEYIOhm_EWnhzEOZKmTtnV3k2h4V2x_O3H7rdW2az5rgktBwzxQ8Ml9Wgkmv85qW-UsBonL4KRa0bcNqdICU6T3NBDdNIZG7w7wEWg4KlnAF-QgF_di6iuT3eEBzh8yrHlFSuLHuhPb0C_IILIr9otWwjpf1KA5GFOSYyQD6gOeKhWj3CG3eZm5p1gmmy40mwStQzVYOOduIKyQjp5IbwqkvVfzuRHAH8XrznnMGZ2eN1aKhHDPUMxKURNCvdo10STsMhPn0BC3lVqqBlf9NjsTRIG8-YozBfNdvJ8kFUuDM2C3JZLXVH5DbtXha7nD98Lzy8Oui-ToNFTQg/4jv/4v-HoXuPS_qaaHZvgJOlWQ/h0/h001.TPZwLI0TwPjtZUp1bTIABMcok_gKKqca6rS0Z9zbfz8)
---
Friend, I’m being targeted for speaking up against the genocide of my people in Palestine. As the only Palestinian American in Congress, I’m a daily reminder to my colleagues that Palestinians are human beings who deserve to live and grow up and experience freedom and safety. **Because I’ve called out my fellow colleagues for their willingness to fund genocide and war crimes, a censure resolution has been introduced against me.** My Republican colleagues don’t see Palestinians, Black people, or other marginalized folks as full humans whose lives matter. **Let’s show them that we belong in the halls of power, and we won’t let them silence us or stop our efforts to save lives**. **** **[Can you push back against attempts to silence our movement by donating to my re-election campaign today?](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqva5xURJUBsgLQSXSHOrodfsJEMWN8ifQ641UqG2ZrJVD3LrOzpIY_W5iuj54hDdJ_uN1jlJIUgJr_QarD_MsDXBCWUp5_Z2KfzB9WUnm_bMXWIKOLi0qgbWMcdOi4A5r_2hSvT6qZU3rH1vo1hYTwElk-MWnFkdTbiUxWmTsoIr5wwBwx935S_S6dPnqoVoBH6xLOvteIXzMk917PFHFlt39KeymljCI-M84OgYKYlviBV5Knh_aaesiiMTFyOMLmQtGUHHNTCHipOjZ6koIQ0tBENMbaZ6Y4eOS7FBPp0VODycxsVMNBz6AYGZ47YckDHKgCMDhg8MWqM-YriTfgsBG3zcG15bvQwEaxomCQ0Mx2iNtwYCdk6-M30Ar8tdfpzlp-7cbQiEyInpRWLCSiw/4jv/4v-HoXuPS_qaaHZvgJOlWQ/h1/h001.pUlTSOCN7M8ZuXzmXvVnOpWNDiVJHmcPPgYl1tSnT4Q)** | [DONATE NOW](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqva5xURJUBsgLQSXSHOrodfsJEMWN8ifQ641UqG2ZrJVH3ZFbw106gfMwg2irQKa9v5uWyJBAvjrRouvfEnUKgl-fkUvv6mg86UU4PtN6nWWPsvMyiT2ru9wf-xXNbNGU5qIWDTTB-K5vlmTPwXaR8g6LIMUMFs4vSmYWB-77bgGxX7Y1F5ha0Q8fOqMPIZPG4TTT81MxagPVDmaP7E4ebSxNLokvG9S--jGZlNIWauxDVNXnmtH5wDekULnnBxQUTL6NOI8Deo4WbyGST22Z4iDkcIL-kX-SWCVMw3OpfWoudhNjjybDoa5zHO7Ki-a7HpH520FRAZ16xWoqYiDZQKZtURPBnTMF3VD91EifrvF0BtGAcRvu4QFitZ1Cu51ftcwDtQWTF1-EHnKv4I1ry9/4jv/4v-HoXuPS_qaaHZvgJOlWQ/h2/h001.86wBlU1-pbxkiFxnwU_G2H7frB_eUw_iWHcNhCBmWgo)
---
**Whether through censure resolutions in Congress or troops in our streets, they are trying to silence dissent. But our movement is growing.**
More Democrats in Congress are swearing off AIPAC funding, and more are supporting policies to restrict U.S. military aid to Israel’s government. At the same time, a new Quinnipiac poll shows that 60% of all U.S. voters oppose sending more military aid to Israel, and half of all voters say Israel is committing genocide against Palestinians in Gaza—with much higher numbers among Democratic voters.1
**I will keep pushing our government to represent the American people and stop the Israeli government’s genocide on Palestinians. I will keep demanding dignity and safety for marginalized people and defending human rights at home and abroad.**
I will continue defending our constitutional rights, including our due process rights and our right to protest against genocide―whether on college campuses, in the streets, or in the halls of Congress.
Republicans can target me for my speech. But I know I have you and millions of people across the country and the world by my side. Let’s show them that together, we won’t stop speaking truth to power.
[**Please donate today to help push back against the GOP’s attempt to censure me and show them they can’t silence our movement to save lives.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqva5xURJUBsgLQSXSHOrodfsJEMWN8ifQ641UqG2ZrJVD3G8rCxFQrfwYq_2Q3fD_Veg9osW_pNj_uy3NJnisSEZvPVGdcy5ZFVvLSk0yoekmloVqq0NfwGTbP2MAGJPceo9awclKsJB2E3EHyKiLoe7aGquAlK0QnDWcablTYjKLIeSi-VR6GS4qt1MCfcrM72TkgIdX4fKrfqUki1CcFWOQkzjh9-I_PKSSUSDH03Wy69DMm2azA36ID_Isnsl2GH9Iti2La0kZf7YTz6tys3P0M1YDX-tMUGzeKr2E_BZu_aQwwxIFD1AO7aQ2YKEB7wGf7N9YfUoW8QjIk72TwsHMGY9Iiqp0nUg4lslq3jI6R7khrBHqSWOmfdhMRE-trlVYQX6FTDhVUXWH88fBca/4jv/4v-HoXuPS_qaaHZvgJOlWQ/h3/h001._Leqrlc4OUmKg2PAl8l5tGTR193ccB8BCORVBZrjAwU)

clean_body: This new censure resolution is an attempt to silence me and our movement for peace. | | | Friend, I’m being targeted for speaking up against the genocide of my people in Palestine. As the only Palestinian American in Congress, I’m a daily reminder to my colleagues that Palestinians are human beings who deserve to live and grow up and experience freedom and safety. Because I’ve called out my fellow colleagues for their willingness to fund genocide and war crimes, a censure resolution has been introduced against me. My Republican colleagues don’t see Palestinians, Black people, or other marginalized folks as full humans whose lives matter. Let’s show them that we belong in the halls of power, and we won’t let them silence us or stop our efforts to save lives. ** | Whether through censure resolutions in Congress or troops in our streets, they are trying to silence dissent. But our movement is growing. More Democrats in Congress are swearing off AIPAC funding, and more are supporting policies to restrict U.S. military aid to Israel’s government. At the same time, a new Quinnipiac poll shows that 60% of all U.S. voters oppose sending more military aid to Israel, and half of all voters say Israel is committing genocide against Palestinians in Gaza—with much higher numbers among Democratic voters.1 I will keep pushing our government to represent the American people and stop the Israeli government’s genocide on Palestinians. I will keep demanding dignity and safety for marginalized people and defending human rights at home and abroad. I will continue defending our constitutional rights, including our due process rights and our right to protest against genocide―whether on college campuses, in the streets, or in the halls of Congress. Republicans can target me for my speech. But I know I have you and millions of people across the country and the world by my side. Let’s show them that together, we won’t stop speaking truth to power. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately: | | | | | | | | | Thank you so much. We aren’t going anywhere! In love and solidarity, Rashida 1 * Rashida Tlaib for Congress PO Box 32777 Detroit, MI 48232 | Paid for by Rashida Tlaib for Congress | | Sent via . body:
The MAGA justices have handed Trump yet another win in his dangerous, authoritarian agenda. | |
---
Peter, **The Supreme Court just told Trump he could cancel millions in public health grants in his anti-DEI crusade:** [****](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm39xlBV8K-h1SPh5Rb04hGqQ83I4vZW-bzPjXm6WUxP_Afug-2wGF3GHkoRXA5d_viECguLfhf6iJCBFO3-FS8gczYfWAClQ_Lw10itbJxmKItTjV_8XAJrHblaZmqk_tP2g0dingCo4J-2AwhoVPFLd994KY9LD7YTylOqA-e26Tbo1w4UQxfBUaHttq76eEZ2DA8swmZ52vfC9e8D450MSmd_G67uWq6WTGgO9MuzRVjfD0va42ZTIsmV4W_2O45xdsdKOA5dJ6HUhPPl1YpjNmKAG9CY_bLAJGFBonXiEnfkx-Cm-1MDaU1aqa_ncwJ06WEuO0TUHRw4RMxeRXQsqx9L1jxX72cSdzFhTWp7dqLrX2VKHNMi2QnFe2f8F6is/4jv/vgdtaW_9RKGg-yRmwgYtEA/h0/h001.aiYNotf9Y0U1ly10pdnW_tx8MAQH_HUfhKUKatd8CUQ) A district judge had previously ruled that Trump cannot wantonly strip public health research funds, saying “I’ve never seen government racial discrimination like this. Have we no shame?”1 **The MAGA justices overturned the judge’s ruling, and Justice Neil Gorsuch even scolded lower courts for daring to intervene.** Now, $783 million in National Institutes of Health research funding is on the chopping block, halting thousands of projects and stalling critical public health breakthroughs. [**The Supreme Court needs to know where We the People stand. Add your name now and tell them, I stand with science, research, and DEI.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm39xlBV8K-h1SPh5Rb04hGqQ83I4vZW-bzPjXm6WUxP_Afug-2wGF3GHkoRXA5d_viECguLfhf6iJCBFO3-FS8gcS7UYoHUzlHcs_Yx-Dr7FYOkrFOgdiCJ1DcnmwEhxRhD98NpbMip9C56DrOtbqmg1Nn0TRUuzN7SIdvFRJ_jxKbPJSp5reSu0V-LrvWye74rU9d25cFzl1HVP9IfiInY5eCxcOAYtB1o6sVi2-OEPnA9FhfYz31DCQ-Fuh9i2eEfdcnf-F9bxcp003_x54zWm8WrMr-PfX3lTcheLCW8AYFfGMFooSxH5YtdqwK2iD2s4I2SfzM_yjbmNy2Qxi4wvC_1UovO3d8QVsQVSJjsPNq0dbSut79id_21xUgRkM9Y/4jv/vgdtaW_9RKGg-yRmwgYtEA/h1/h001.yD8Jfmp_hHPEZpj14I85bhb4FyEEXnWbVdB4BDqU5v4) [ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm39xlBV8K-h1SPh5Rb04hGqQ83I4vZW-bzPjXm6WUxP_Afug-2wGF3GHkoRXA5d_viECguLfhf6iJCBFO3-FS8gc-c6J3NamcGfS6VNjFXEW3v8m2lgxHYNNYez7ZMDImfkHuVXN8xtXNO0O-yHgTMyEDKpAYhAdKZLaXOnhnrNez_zkFz9WJ69jlKRkbcbFhQuVEWF7FWDoVhX9myBxHyXzRF5V_kiUS3MIkM500A7h0v7tiqV1feadp4xWPkqOdwrKJ8V_p09THMpHh8FJscrnqMK01UaMbUA0p7JACdBpYBihnDO04jeSDyBdo_oQN3JmppQZ301vTCaPyE4GByerN9eVBDE3ca6qgxTx9nYoq-nCI6-vfmwAteOvKcqc85A/4jv/vgdtaW_9RKGg-yRmwgYtEA/h2/h001.w6JSe7nADVX4nYJdpM86N8eOuQozUgh0-E99wTO6IHo) **The MAGA crusade has warped the meaning of DEI.** DEI is simply a commitment to diversity, equity, and inclusion. That means ensuring our public health research doesn’t neglect underserved communities, or fails to focus on minority communities that need help. **Trump’s canceling of public health research grants is just racism repackaged.** And the Supreme Court is all too happy to comply. Right-wing extremists have corrupted our justice system to the point where the highest Court in the land can’t be trusted to stand up for the rights and lives of its most vulnerable people. Instead, the Court is solely focused on propping up a wannabe dictator. [**Most Americans support public research to keep our friends, neighbors, and family members healthy. The Supreme Court needs to hear from the millions of people it’s ruled against. Stand with science, research, and DEI. Add your name today!**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm39xlBV8K-h1SPh5Rb04hGqQ83I4vZW-bzPjXm6WUxP_Afug-2wGF3GHkoRXA5d_viECguLfhf6iJCBFO3-FS8gcbwJgGkzLFbZly85WH265ldySC1P1oJtd7DH0OrH0CLArlhFKtcfUQO2SLum8ltHS5ebMU8sA14402OvM4wOfdq6YvhGb6IXQN9-nfyhaMsH31o4hUozDLJsJ1BGdTSy6djdYZipXAUGI-lczk4hgxK_v0OOxc1RATPnGV522pm21aIvd-SkTpfdKU9DxHM9C9bd6WyFYBHPAMAXgP23rqdPpQP1xBmtxE1ihpvzY10pU77eBuDwmWbD3XODxJwuWiaiyOgahhgJntm5qJ4Bt-stFL5pBcrqcf2P4paTnz1I/4jv/vgdtaW_9RKGg-yRmwgYtEA/h3/h001.m2GXvwalByqEG-_g5I3SNi7sDjg8jGWE4C25a_1Huak) Thank you for supporting public health research for our most vulnerable, Morgan Stahr

# _Opinion:_
**By Anon and The Capitalist Staff**
In footage circulating across social media, a young woman beams into her phone camera, her expression one of unalloyed delight--as if recounting a promotion or a child's first steps. _**"The best part about this whole goddamn thing"**_ she begins, her voice bubbling with glee, "_**Is that he is not martyr material so his death will mean nothing."**_
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/c0f7fcff-3ed6-4b77-b30c-ed920b48f3e7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Only when the audio clarifies does the horror land: This is no tale of triumph, but a young woman, the type you would see anywhere in this nation celebrating the assassination of a political adversary.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/d1f8b552-f84d-4b42-8fe5-8182f49a3e0b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The video, on her now private account, captured in the hours after Charlie Kirk's fatal shooting on September 10, 2025, at Utah Valley University, will be seen millions of times, reposted across the globe. She ends the video ecstatically _**" It's just great."**_ The damage has been done--not just to her reputation and career, which will likely unravel in the cascade of firings and public shaming that follow, -- but to the fragile illusion that political enmity stops short of bloodlust.
**A message from our sponsor (piece continues below)**
* * *
* * *
**[In Partnership with Native Path](https://substack.com/redirect/3352a0cf-59a5-44cc-b6bc-e0c6855673c6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[ ](https://substack.com/redirect/3352a0cf-59a5-44cc-b6bc-e0c6855673c6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/3352a0cf-59a5-44cc-b6bc-e0c6855673c6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Friend, picture this with me:
Your physician **grinning** over your next blood test results...
**Never again** having to worry about the **long bathroom lines that might cause an embarrassing leak ...**
Or the **muscle spasms waking you up throughout the night ...**
What's more: enjoying **energy, strength, and stamina** so strong, even your grandkids can't keep up with you.
I'm even willing to bet your brain will be firing on all cylinders, with memory and social skills so strong that your family and friends will be asking what you're doing differently!
_**Best of all**_ ...
**[THIS"salty" drink](https://substack.com/redirect/3352a0cf-59a5-44cc-b6bc-e0c6855673c6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** is completely natural and safe... and based on study participant results, can start working in as little as 2 hours!
**[>>> Discover the 7 reasons everyone born before 1973 needs THIS "salty" drink](https://substack.com/redirect/3352a0cf-59a5-44cc-b6bc-e0c6855673c6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
[Learn More](https://substack.com/redirect/3352a0cf-59a5-44cc-b6bc-e0c6855673c6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
* * *
**(Piece continues)**
Kirk, the 31-year-old founder of Turning Point USA, was no stranger to controversy. A Trump ally who built a multimillion-dollar empire mobilizing young conservatives, he thrived on debate, often inviting liberal interlocutors onstage for pointed but civil exchanges. His death by a sniper's bullet to the neck during an outdoor speech attended by 3,000 students, shattered that ethos.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a158e865-9b20-4476-8135-afc7b4883e72?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The suspect, 22-year-old Tyler Robinson from St. George, Utah, surrendered to authorities on September 12 after his family, tipped off by FBI photos, persuaded him to turn himself in. It is still early but Robinson appears to have become increasingly politicized and radicalized by the left, opposed to Kirk's views, he had reportedly etched extremist slogans on the bullet casings. The killing, unfolding in broad daylight before a crowd, evoking a grim echo the assassinations of the 1960s.
Yet as wrenching as the act itself was the aftermath: a torrent of online jubilation from corners of the left once dismissed as fringe. Blue-haired avatars and pronoun-laden bios have long served as caricatures of progressive excess, but this was different.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/73cd69ec-c45e-4517-9101-e86d8b154601?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The celebrants were often unremarkable individuals--teachers, nurses, even members of the military. In a matter of hours, dozens of such individuals lost their jobs, their gleeful tweets and TikToks doxxed and dissected and delivered to their employers by horrified readers. What emerged was not the cartoonish radical, but a new archetype: the "Stealth Woke." Professionals whose outward normalcy concealed a venom that spilled unchecked into public view. It even extends to Robinson himself. Ask anybody to come up with who they thought would eventually be responsible for Kirk's execution and very few would have thought they would look like this.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d356d33f-fe98-4835-8a94-fa99aea5616f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
This phenomenon demands scrutiny, not schadenfreude. It reveals a cultural rot deeper than many can comprehend, where the mental guardrails of human decency have eroded to the point of nonexistence. Kirk embodied the American ideal of open discourse--he was, to the end, a husband and a father who preached dialogue over division. His willingness to engage, even with protesters of him and his events, underscored a faith in reason's redemptive power. That faith now feels quaint amid a landscape where disagreement metastasizes into the crack of a rifle.
Consider the mechanics of this stealth radicalism. Social media, with its algorithmic echo chambers, has long amplified extremes, but the past decade has normalized a rhetoric of dehumanization. On the left, this manifests in portrayals of conservatives not as misguided peers, but as irredeemable villains--fascists, enablers of white supremacy, obstacles to justice, "Nazis". Kirk, with his critiques of campus "woke" culture and defenses of Second Amendment rights, became a lightning rod. Online harassment of conservative figures like Kirk is often laced with violent imagery.
But the "Stealth Woke" transcend memes and trolls. They are embedded in institutions, their views simmering beneath professional veneers. The girl in the video, radiated the affability of trust; her body language, sans sound, could pass for innocuous joy. Play the audio, and the dissonance chills: a gleeful endorsement of political murder, posted without apparent hesitation.
* * *
_**" That might be what's most crushing, sure you'll find psychos online who are clearly down deep in the hole of evil, we've all seen that, but the ones who look like "normies" celebrating such an act is deeply disturbing." \- Anonymous**_
* * *
Similar cases proliferated. A high school teacher was placed on leave by her district for her posts. A U.S. Army soldier prompted an investigation by the Department of Defense for theirs. These were not anonymous "edgelords", but "normies"--adults with mortgages, PTA meetings to go to, resumes and relationships--who bypassed the internal whisper of "Should I really post this?" Their firings, while swift, underscore a deeper malaise: When did celebrating death become not just tolerable, but instinctive?
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2f24795e-ac58-49b8-973c-a73d2da11a3d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
This is no mere left-wing pathology; it's a mirror to broader societal fractures. The left's "Stealth Woke" variant stings for its subversion of empathy, that supposed hallmark of progressive virtue.
Historical parallels abound: In the 1960s assassinations of figures like Robert Kennedy and MLK fueled cycles of recrimination, eroding trust in institutions. The "Stealth Woke" accelerate this, blurring lines between private resentment and public incitement. Why post publicly? Perhaps the dopamine of likes, or the illusion of safety in numbers. More troublingly, it suggests a desensitization where Kirk's humanity--his two young children, his devotion to family--registers as irrelevant collateral.
Yet amid the outrage, glimmers of accountability emerge. Employers, from hospitals to school boards, have acted decisively, reminding us that norms endure when enforced. Robinson's family, in turning him in, modeled a quiet heroism: a pastor and father bridging the chasm of radicalization. These acts hint at reclamation, a refusal to let the "Stealth Woke" define the discourse.
As the nation grapples with Kirk's absence--his casket borne on Air Force Two by allies like JD Vance--we must confront what his death unmasks. Not the stereotypes of dyed hair and piercings, but the insidious creep of rage into everyday lives.
America is at a crossroads, If it is to heal, it demands more than finger-pointing: it demands a reckoning with how the beasts of division have been fed. Will we rebuild the table of dialogue Kirk so cherished, or let stealth radicalism claim the public square?
The answer lies not in vengeance, but in vigilance--over our words, our feeds, and the quiet erosions within.
For in celebrating a death, we diminish our own.
* * *

Peter,

Something big is happening in this race.

Not long ago, the establishment-backed candidate was ahead of us by double
digits. After multiple rallies and 8,000+ miles traveled throughout
Michigan, we’ve narrowed it down to just two points:

[ We're ]We're Surging! Stevens - 24%. El-Sayed - 22%. McMorrow - 11%

It’s clear what we’re doing is working.

And it’s inspiring to see — a fully people-powered campaign going
toe-to-toe with one bankrolled by corporate PACs, AIPAC, and the
Democratic establishment.

Now, we just have to keep this momentum going.

name: Urgent Action via MGP for Congress
subject: Now is the time to step up
body:
| | Peter,

You've heard from Marie, and now we're following up.

**Our FEC deadline is _not_ just important; it's urgent. We need your support now more than ever.**

What we report will set the direction for the months ahead in the most consequential election of our generation. The results right here in WA-03 will determine whether Congress remains in the grip of a dysfunctional, bickering Majority that bows to extremists.

Holding this top five competitive seat in the face of attacks from far-right extremists and dark money groups means ensuring our fundraising stays on track.

_We must be able to compete to win, Peter._

However, we're currently $14,700 short of where we need to be in September. If we don't close the gap, then it will be increasingly difficult to make up the difference in the months ahead.

And that will give Marie's opponent the advantage.

[Can you pitch in immediately, Peter, to help us make up for lost ground and put us on an equal footing?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115159251/569381183/-1068804189?amount=10&refcode=MGP_EM_FR_90DO_250912_2_2&refcode2=99e30bc9-bd90-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTkyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5OWUzMGJjOS1iZDkwLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=oModvnqvPHSIV8y07N7hJNi1OZujIObr_fHjRPP_qRA=) _If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately._ | | **[DONATE $10 NOW »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115159254/569381187/-1068804189?amount=10&express_lane=true&refcode=MGP_EM_FR_90DO_250912_2_2&refcode2=99e30bc9-bd90-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTkyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5OWUzMGJjOS1iZDkwLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=oModvnqvPHSIV8y07N7hJNi1OZujIObr_fHjRPP_qRA=)**

name: Chris Pappas
subject: John E. Sununu’s record speaks for itself
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TAMMY BALDWIN FOR SENATE
body:
| This is urgent.
# [](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/67?t=1&akid=4975%2E35263%2E9rZwQX)
Peter, Chris Pappas here. **National Republicans are recruiting John E. Sununu for U.S. Senate here in NH — and I need you to know what that means.** Sununu represented New Hampshire in Congress nearly _twenty years ago._ And throughout his time in DC, he put special interests ahead of Granite Staters every chance he got. **_His record speaks for itself_****— he voted against lowering drug prices, repeatedly voted to restrict abortion access, and led efforts to privatize Social Security.** And what has he done since leaving office? Joined a lobbying firm that represented corporate special interests. Now he’s national Republicans’ handpicked candidate for this race. With name recognition like his, this is an all-hands-on-deck moment. **[Please split $10 today between my campaign and Tammy’s to stop national Republicans from buying this Senate seat.](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/2533?c0=stb-partner_ms_cp_em_baldwin.OcyrX_&refcode=AU_STB_FR_TAN_GEN_AC_20250913_PM1_V1_S1&t=4&akid=4975%2E35263%2E9rZwQX)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation to Tammy Baldwin and Chris Pappas will go through immediately:_ | | [CHIP IN $10 NOW](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/3664?c0=stb-partner_ms_cp_em_baldwin.XM_kl8&refcode=AU_STB_FR_TAN_GEN_AC_20250913_PM1_V1_S1&t=5&akid=4975%2E35263%2E9rZwQX)

subject: re: Redistricting in CA

Peter,

In this email, we’re going to ask you to chip in **[$3](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115160540/569383880/-1384072709?refcode=em-9.13.25-all-b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDE1MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2NWE2MjUyYS1jMTkwLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=4YYxQOvhase61o9jQE3PAKy01mqQlGlgXlQz0dm9vCo=)** to support George’s re-election effort. Things are likely to change significantly in our race. Let us explain why that is, and why your support is so urgent.

As you may have heard, California is preparing to redraw its congressional maps in response to Texas’ plot to add more GOP House seats to hold the House majority.

**If Prop 50 passes this fall, George’s district could add thousands of new voters -- meaning we’ll be campaigning in brand-new areas and introducing George to communities he hasn’t represented before.**

That’s a huge opportunity, but also poses a challenge.

We’ll need to expand our outreach and scale up our campaign virtually overnight to make sure our message reaches new voters who will be hearing from George for the very first time.That kind of rapid growth takes resources -- and we need to start preparing now.

**[This is the final FEC deadline before the new maps could take effect, and what we raise now will determine if we have the resources to meet the moment. Will you chip in $3 to make sure we’re ready?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115160541/569383881/-1384072709?refcode=em-9.13.25-all-b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDE1MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2NWE2MjUyYS1jMTkwLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=4YYxQOvhase61o9jQE3PAKy01mqQlGlgXlQz0dm9vCo=)**

name: Gabby Giffords
subject: I’m asking you to do something important today
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
#

Team,
Gabby Giffords here. It’s Saturday, September 13 — and today, I am asking you to do something special.
[**Today, I am asking you to make your first online donation to GIFFORDS to help save more lives from gun violence.**](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-sep25.vnrwKJ&refcode=em250913-2-b&t=1&akid=10861%2E3935704%2E6qHuyc)
Over the past 12 years, we’ve made so much progress in taking on the gun lobby and changing the way politicians think about gun safety. Many used to lack the courage to do what is right. Today, gun safety is a winning issue.
The contributions made from grassroots supporters in Cresco and across the country have made a big difference in our progress. And while I wish I didn’t have to ask you to donate today, this is important.
GIFFORDS is working to elect real gun safety champions and stop the gun violence epidemic across the country...but we can’t do it without you.
Only about 2% of people who open fundraising emails choose to make a contribution. That’s why, today, I am asking you to join that special group by making your first online donation.
[**Please use this link to make your first online donation to GIFFORDS PAC. Our average donation is around $25. Some people give more — many less. What matters most is that you choose to join the fight before our mid-month deadline in 48 hours.**](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-sep25.vnrwKJ&refcode=em250913-2-b&t=2&akid=10861%2E3935704%2E6qHuyc)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_
| | **[Donate $3](https://go.giffords.org/go/17015?c0=giffords-em-sep25.LcBf1X&amount=3&refcode=em250913-2-b&t=21&akid=10861%2E3935704%2E6qHuyc)**

| **[Donate $25](https://go.giffords.org/go/17017?c0=giffords-em-sep25.C0zaJv&refcode=em250913-2-b&t=22&akid=10861%2E3935704%2E6qHuyc)**
---
****
| | **[Donate $50](https://go.giffords.org/go/17010?c0=giffords-em-sep25.OgxiUJ&refcode=em250913-2-b&t=23&akid=10861%2E3935704%2E6qHuyc)**
---
****
| **[Donate $100](https://go.giffords.org/go/17011?c0=giffords-em-sep25.HTMwGp&refcode=em250913-2-b&t=24&akid=10861%2E3935704%2E6qHuyc)**
---
****
| | **[Donate $250](https://go.giffords.org/go/17012?c0=giffords-em-sep25.fqUxQD&refcode=em250913-2-b&t=25&akid=10861%2E3935704%2E6qHuyc)**
---
****
| **[Other Amount](https://go.giffords.org/go/17015?c0=giffords-em-sep25.LcBf1X&refcode=em250913-2-b&t=26&akid=10861%2E3935704%2E6qHuyc)**
---
****
With courage,
Gabby Gifford
**[DONATE](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-sep25.vnrwKJ&refcode=em250913-2-b&t=27&akid=10861%2E3935704%2E6qHuyc)**

Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. www.Giffords.org
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. We try to send only the most important information and opportunities to participate via email, but click [here](https://go.giffords.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=28&akid=10861%2E3935704%2E6qHuyc) if you'd like to unsubscribe. Giffords PAC was started to support elected officials who step up to fight the gun violence epidemic. Thank you for standing with Gabby and the Giffords team.

subject: set aside the polls
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Jones for Virginia

We’re running against someone who has a dystopian view of what Virginia should look like. &| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115160573/569384069/338076930?source=LOC_EM_FR_2025.09.13_PollsPundits_B1_F1_S1_C1_U2_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDg2MTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNTQ5ZGQ4YWQtYzE5MC1mMDExLWI0ODQtNjA0NWJkZWI3NDEzIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=0OaMx3DxZUiCUoh-0IPnp3QXBrf_AnWdJnso-pI1qLM=)

| | Hi, Jay Jones here.For a minute, I want you to set aside what you’ve heard about our race – whether it’s the polls, or what your favorite pundit has to say. Because here’s the reality – we’re running against someone who has a dystopian view of what Virginia should look like.**That includes abortion bans, siding with corrupt special interests over small businesses, and using his power as Attorney General to do Donald Trump’s bidding.** I’m not just being facetious, folks. Jason Miyares’ record speaks volumes about the Virginia that he wants families like yours and mine to live in.That’s not a risk we can take in November, and we need to make every effort to ensure Miyares doesn’t get a second chance at crossing these items off his list. [**Your support means everything to campaigns like ours, so can I count on you to chip in $25 or more to help us defeat Jason Miyares on November 4th?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115160575/569384071/338076930?amount=25&source=LOC_EM_FR_2025.09.13_PollsPundits_B1_F1_S1_C1_U2_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDg2MTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNTQ5ZGQ4YWQtYzE5MC1mMDExLWI0ODQtNjA0NWJkZWI3NDEzIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=0OaMx3DxZUiCUoh-0IPnp3QXBrf_AnWdJnso-pI1qLM=)

clean_body: We’re running against someone who has a dystopian view of what Virginia should look like. &| | | = | | Hi, Jay Jones here.For a minute, I want you to set aside what you’ve heard about our race – whether it’s the polls, or what your favorite pundit has to say. Because here’s the reality – we’re running against someone who has a dystopian view of what Virginia should look like.That includes abortion bans, siding with corrupt special interests over small businesses, and using his power as Attorney General to do Donald Trump’s bidding. I’m not just being facetious, folks. Jason Miyares’ record speaks volumes about the Virginia that he wants families like yours and mine to live in.That’s not a risk we can take in November, and we need to make every effort to ensure Miyares doesn’t get a second chance at crossing these items off his list. = = = = = = = With you, \- Jay Jay Jones for Virginia 1490-5A Quarterpath Road, #195 Williamsburg, VA 23185 United States | | | Paid for by Jay Jones for Virginia | | | If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please .

name: Binkley for Congress
subject: Last chance to RSVP for Sunday’s launch!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ryan Binkley for Congress
body:
I don’t want you to miss it.

| | | | | |
---
| | Dear Friend,
This is it. Our campaign launch is happening this Sunday, and I don’t want you to miss it.
We’ll gather at the UT-Dallas Davidson-Gundy Alumni Center to share our vision for Texas and America, and to officially kick off a campaign focused on faith, family, freedom, and opportunity.**UT-Dallas – Davidson-Gundy Alumni Center
** **Sunday, September 14
** **4:00 – 5:30pm CDT**
If you haven’t reserved your ticket yet, please do so now:

| [RSVP HERE](https://binkleyforcongress.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a7930d2727540612917fd641e&id=0705ada5f1&e=a4fe331f26)

| | I look forward to seeing you on Sunday as we start this journey together.
With gratitude,Ryan Binkley
P.S. Your donation of even just $5 today can help power our campaign and help flip this seat RED. [Can we count on your end-of-quarter donation here?](https://binkleyforcongress.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a7930d2727540612917fd641e&id=b74cf53061&e=a4fe331f26)

| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T820190ea-408f-4e2a-8ce8-a9ccaf6a6513/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

name: Team Guzman
subject: All Eyes on Virginia
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Elizabeth Guzman
body:
Some say our election this November is in an off year... | | | **** Friend,
Some say our election this November is an “off year.” **We don’t believe in “off years”... especially when****the whole country is watching.** That’s because if we flip this seat, we can expand our 51-49 Democratic majority in the Virginia House of Delegates and prove voters are ready to protect rights and reject extremism in 2026. But if we lose, **Republicans will claim momentum heading into 2026**. **_And we cannot let that happen.

clean_body: Some say our election this November is in an off year... | | | ** Friend, Some say our election this November is an “off year.” We don’t believe in “off years”... especially when**the whole country is watching. That’s because if we flip this seat, we can expand our 51-49 Democratic majority in the Virginia House of Delegates and prove voters are ready to protect rights and reject extremism in 2026. But if we lose, Republicans will claim momentum heading into 2026. And we cannot let that happen. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately. Virginia’s election in November will set the tone for the midterms in 2026, and as Virginia goes, so goes the nation. Early voting starts in less than two weeks , and we need every resource to win. — Team Guzman | Democrat is running for Delegate in Virginia's 22nd District. She came to America as a single mother, becoming a union leader and one of the first Latinas in the Virginia General Assembly. She passed stronger gun safety laws, expanded collective bargaining rights and paid sick leave, and protected reproductive rights. To donate by check, send to: Friends of Elizabeth Guzman PO Box 74, Nokesville VA, 20182 \- \- Paid for by Friends of Elizabeth Guzman. Contributions or gifts to Guzman for Congress are not deductible as charitable contributions for federal income tax purposes. | | Sent via .
